data.content:
Here's my analysis. I'll now modernize the project.

**Legacy patterns identified:**

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Simplification |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | Replace with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `<Import>` of props/targets | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults | SDK provides these |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults |
| 6 | Debug/Release PropertyGroups | All standard defaults |
| 7 | `<Reference Include="System.*">` | Implicit for .NET Framework |
| 8 | `<Compile Include="...">` | Auto-globbing includes all `.cs` |
| 9 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Metadata moves into `.csproj` properties |
| 10 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `<Import>` of Microsoft.Common.props and Microsoft.CSharp.targets** — handled implicitly by SDK
3. **Explicit `Configuration`/`Platform` conditions** with defaults — SDK provides these by default
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`FileAlignment`**, **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`**, and **`Deterministic`** — all default values in SDK-style
6. **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** with standard settings — unnecessary since SDK handles these defaults
7. **Explicit `<Reference>` items for System assemblies** — implicit in .NET Framework or unnecessary when targeting .NET 6+
8. **Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items** — SDK-style automatically includes all .cs files via glob patterns
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes directly in the .csproj instead
10. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — I'll update this to `TargetFramework` with `net472` or consider targeting a modern .NET version

Now I'm going to modernize the project file.

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (all handled by SDK defaults):**
- XML declaration, `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`
- `<Import>` of props/targets
- `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`
- Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (standard defaults)
- Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items (auto-globbing)
- `<Reference Include="System.*">` (implicit for `net472`)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (metadata now in `<PropertyGroup>`)

**Kept/converted:**
- `OutputType` dropped — `Library` is the SDK default
- `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`
- Assembly attributes → `Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version` properties
